docs.title: Ontology for Traffic Regulations
docs.text:
This ontology defines concepts related to traffic regulations using concepts specified in ISO/IEC 5087-1 and ISO/IEC-5087-2.

This ontology consists of the following patterns:

  • Tro Pattern

The formal definition of these patterns is available in OWL Functional Notation.

docs.text: Property Value Restriction Definition rdfs:subClassOf TrafficControlDevice ---

docs.title: LegalBasis
docs.text:
A legal basis provides references to the legal documents that authorize an entity to issue the types of regulations covered by a traffic regulation order.

Open interactive LegalBasis diagram

docs.title: Formalization for LegalBasis
docs.text: Property Value Restriction Definition date max 1 xsd::date None date only xsd::date None genProp::hasName exactly 1 xsd::string None genProp::hasName only xsd::string None rdfs:subClassOf TroThing --- version max 1 xsd::string None version only xsd::string None

docs.title: RuleMaker
docs.text:
A rule maker is an entity that has the responsibility for creating and maintaining rules of the road or regulations for a geographic and operational scope as defined by the parent jurisdictional entity.

Open interactive RuleMaker diagram

docs.title: Formalization for RuleMaker
docs.text: Property Value Restriction Definition legalBasis max 1 LegalBasis formal authority within the law (e.g., statute, ordinance, administrative rule, constitutional provision) that empowers an entity to create, adopt, or enforce a regulation rdfs:subClassOf TroThing ---

docs.title: TrafficRegulation
docs.text:
A traffic regulation is a rule having the force of law that is established by a regulator through a traffic regulation order.

Open interactive TrafficRegulation diagram

docs.title: Formalization for TrafficRegulation
docs.text: Property Value Restriction Definition associatedTrafficControlDevice only TrafficControlDevice None rdfs:subClassOf TroThing ---

docs.title: TrafficRegulationOrder
docs.text:
A TrafficRegulation Order is a legally recognised document that specifies one or more traffic regulations.

Open interactive TrafficRegulationOrder diagram

docs.title: Tro Pattern
docs.text:
It consists of the following classes:

  • Access Control Device
  • Channelization Device
  • Legal Basis
  • Pavement Marking
  • Road Sign
  • Road Surface Feature
  • Rule Maker
  • Traffic Control Device
  • Traffic Regulation
  • Traffic Regulation Order
  • Traffic Signal
  • Traffic Signal Device
  • Tro Thing
  • Warning Beacon

docs.title: Specializations of TroThing
docs.text: Class Description Access Control Device Channelization Device Legal Basis A legal basis provides references to the legal documents that authorize an entity to issue the types of regulations covered by a traffic regulation order. Pavement Marking Road Sign Road Surface Feature Rule Maker A rule maker is an entity that has the responsibility for creating and maintaining rules of the road or regulations for a geographic and operational scope as defined by the parent jurisdictional entity. Traffic Control Device Traffic Regulation A traffic regulation is a rule having the force of law that is established by a regulator through a traffic regulation order. Traffic Regulation Order A TrafficRegulation Order is a legally recognised document that specifies one or more traffic regulations. Traffic Signal Traffic Signal Device Warning Beacon
